preamble: The shift from the high back rounded vowel [u] to the mid front unrounded vowel [e] is a complex process involving fronting and lowering, often occurring as part of a vowel chain shift or through an intermediate rounded front vowel [y].

examples.note: Some MHG ü/y forms appear with /e/ reflex in certain Yiddish dialects; the front rounded ü unrounded via intermediate stages to e or i depending on dialect and phonetic context.
Historical fronting to [y] followed by lowering and unrounding to [e] in specific regional varieties.
Part of the Great Vowel Shift chain where high vowels diphthongized and interacted with fronted reflexes.
